---
title: "CH-7"
author: "Tawhid Tasdique"
date: "2026-04-04"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

pkg <- c("tidyverse",
         "ggthemes",
         "rvest",
         "gridExtra",
         "MASS",
         "mvtnorm")

lapply(X = pkg, FUN = library, character.only = T)

set.seed(27)

```

# Fig-7.1

We've been advised to `MASS::mvrnorm` and `stats::rWishart` for simulation purposes. 

The aim here is to replicate the same contour with varying degrees of correlation.

```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

set.seed(27)


dist1 <- 40:60 # same range used in textbook
#####
# # uncorrelated
# dist2 <- mvrnorm(
#   n = 30,
#   # sample size
#   mu = c(50, 50),
#   # mean vector
#   Sigma = c(64, 0, 0, 144) %>% # error: vector was provided
#     matrix(nrow = 2,               # fix: always provide matrix
#            byrow = T),
#   # the covariance matrix
#   empirical = F
# ) # these are population parameters
# 
# # positively correlated
# dist3 <- mvrnorm(
#   n = 30,
#   # sample size
#   mu = c(50, 50),
#   # mean vector
#   Sigma = c(64, 48, 48, 144) %>% # error: vector was provided
#     matrix(nrow = 2,               # fix: always provide matrix
#            byrow = T),
#   # the covariance matrix
#   empirical = F
# ) # these are population parameters
#####

# combined plot
tibble(
  y1 = dist1 %>% rep(x = ., each = length(dist1)), 
  y2 = dist1 %>% rep(x = ., times = length(dist1)), 
  pdf = 
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = y1, y = y2)) +
  geom_contour(aes(z = dmvnorm()))

```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(mvtnorm)

set.seed(27)
dist1 <- 40:60

# Define parameters
mu <- c(50, 50)
Sigma1 <- matrix(c(64, -48, -48, 144), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
Sigma2 <- matrix(c(64, 0, 0, 144), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
Sigma3 <- matrix(c(64, 48, 48, 144), nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)

# Create the grid and calculate PDF
plot_data <- tibble(
  y1 = rep(x = dist1, each = length(dist1)), 
  y2 = rep(x = dist1, times = length(dist1))
) %>%
  mutate(
    # dmvnorm expects a matrix or data frame of coordinates
    pdf1 = dmvnorm(x = cbind(y1, y2), mean = mu, sigma = Sigma1), 
    pdf2 = dmvnorm(x = cbind(y1, y2), mean = mu, sigma = Sigma2),
    pdf3 = dmvnorm(x = cbind(y1, y2), mean = mu, sigma = Sigma3)
  )

# negatively correlated
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = y1, y = y2, z = pdf1)) +
  geom_contour(aes(color = after_stat(level)), 
               linewidth = 1.5) + # color codes the isobars
  coord_fixed() + # Keeps the aspect ratio 1:1
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Bivariate Normal Contour Plot",
       x = expression(theta[1]),
       y = expression(theta[2]))

# uncorrelated
p2 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = y1, y = y2, z = pdf2)) +
  geom_contour(aes(color = after_stat(level)), 
               linewidth = 1.5) + # color codes the isobars
  coord_fixed() + # Keeps the aspect ratio 1:1
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Bivariate Normal Contour Plot",
       x = expression(theta[1]),
       y = expression(theta[2]))

# positively correlated
p3 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = y1, y = y2, z = pdf3)) +
  geom_contour(aes(color = after_stat(level)), 
               linewidth = 1.5) + # color codes the isobars
  coord_fixed() + # Keeps the aspect ratio 1:1
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Bivariate Normal Contour Plot",
       x = expression(theta[1]),
       y = expression(theta[2]))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, nrow = 1)

```

# Pg-112: Reading Comprehension

```{r}

# hyper-parameters for theta
mu0 <- c(50, 50)
L0 <- c(625, 312.5, 312.5, 625) %>%
  matrix(nrow = 2, byrow = T)

# hyper-parameters for sigma
nu0 <- 4 
# since we're not very confident about Sigma0, we let nu0 = p + 2

# S0 = L0, is deliberately assumed (because it's not reliable enough)
S0 <- c(625, 312.5, 312.5, 625) %>% # S0 = Sigma0, low-trust prior 
  matrix(nrow = 2, byrow = T)

Y.reading <- structure(.Data = c(59, 43, 34, 32, 42, 38, 55, 67, 64, 45, 49, 72, 34, 70, 34, 50, 41, 52, 60, 34, 28, 35, 77, 39, 46, 26, 38, 43, 68, 86, 77, 60, 50, 59, 38, 48, 55, 58, 54, 60, 75, 47, 48, 33), 
                       .Dim = c(22L, 2L), 
                       .Dimnames = list(NULL, c("pretest", "posttest")))

Y <- Y.reading

n <- Y %>% nrow(); ybar <- colMeans(Y)

# sample covariance matrix
Sigma <- cov(Y)

# objects for storage purpose
THETA <- SIGMA <- NULL

# setting seed
set.seed(27)

# for loop for updating parameters
for(i in 1:5000){
  
  # posterior parameters for location vector
  mun <- solve(n*solve(Sigma) + solve(L0)) %*% (solve(L0)%*%mu0 + n*solve(Sigma)%*%ybar)
  Ln <- solve(n*solve(Sigma) + solve(L0))
  
  # sampling location vector
  theta <- mvtnorm::rmvnorm(n = 1, # we only need one random vector
                            mean = mun, 
                            sigma = Ln) # note: this is a row vector
  
  # update location vector
  THETA[[i]] <- theta
  
  # residual sum of squares matrix given a prior location vector
  S_theta <- (t(Y) - c(theta)) %*% t(t(Y) - c(theta))
  # I'm still concerned about how the above expression is carrying out the intended operation. Don't worry, it works, it's legit.
  
  # posterior parameters for covariance matrix
  Sn <- S0 + S_theta
  
  # posterior degrees of freedom
  nun <- nu0 + n
  
  # sampling covariance matrix
  sampled_sigma <- rWishart(
    n = 1,   
    df = nun, 
    Sigma = solve(Sn)
  ) %>% 
    as.numeric() %>%
    matrix(nrow = 2, byrow = T) %>% 
    solve() 
  # otherwise you have an information matrix, not what you need
  
  # update covariance matrix
  # double brackets are essential for convenient access
  SIGMA[[i]] <- sampled_sigma
  # when you were using list(), you had the Matryoshka problem
  
}

# storing difference in locations of before and after 
del_theta <- sapply(X = 1:length(THETA), 
                    FUN = function(i){
                      
                      THETA[[i]] %>% as.numeric() %>% diff()
                    
                      })

# proportion of positive increments in comprehension after intervention
mean(del_theta > 0)

```

## FIG-7.2

```{r}

theta1 <- sapply(X = 1:length(THETA), 
                  FUN = function(i){
                    THETA[[i]][1]
                  })

theta2 <- sapply(X = 1:length(THETA), 
                  FUN = function(i){
                    THETA[[i]][2]
                  })

# 1. Calculate posterior estimates from your MCMC samples
post_mu <- colMeans(cbind(theta1, theta2))
post_sigma <- cov(cbind(theta1, theta2))

# 2. Create a regular grid for the contour to "walk" on
# grid_range <- seq(min(theta1)-5, max(theta1)+5, length.out = 100)
grid <- expand.grid(
  theta1 = seq(min(theta1) - 5, max(theta1) + 5, length.out = 100),
  theta2 = seq(min(theta2) - 5, max(theta2) + 5, length.out = 100)
)

# 3. Calculate PDF over that grid
plot_data <- grid %>%
  mutate(pdf = dmvnorm(as.matrix(grid), mean = post_mu, sigma = post_sigma))

# 4. Plot
ggplot(data = plot_data, 
       mapping = aes(x = theta1, y = theta2, z = pdf)) +
  geom_contour(mapping = aes(color = after_stat(level)),# plots isobars 
               linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, 
              intercept = 0) +
  # Optional: Overlay the actual MCMC points to see if they fit!
  geom_point(data = tibble(theta1, theta2), 
             mapping = aes(z = NULL), 
             alpha = 0.3, 
             size = 0.5, 
             color = "red") +
  coord_fixed() + # what does this do?? fixes the aspect ratio
  theme_dark() +
  scale_color_viridis_c() + # color-scheme for color-blind people
  labs(title = "Posterior Joint Density of Theta",
       subtitle = "Contours calculated from MCMC posterior covariance",
       x = expression(theta[1]), 
       y = expression(theta[2]))

```
